articles.title: Jaleel asks Fadnavis to resign over failing law and order in state
articles.description: AIMIM's Imtiaz Jaleel slammed the Maharashtra govt and CM Fadnavis for failing to maintain law and order. He said if the Home Minister can't ensure safety, he should resign, and if police can't keep peace, they should "sit at home".

articles.title: AAP wiped out in Rajya Sabha: BJP's Shahnawaz Hussain on defections
articles.description: BJP leader Shahnawaz Hussain claims AAP is 'wiped out' in the Rajya Sabha after its MPs defected. This comes as AAP's Sanjay Singh petitioned the Rajya Sabha Chairman to disqualify 7 MPs who joined BJP, challenging the merger's legality.

articles.title: MP CM Mohan Yadav praises PM Modi's 'Mann Ki Baat' for awareness
articles.description: MP CM Mohan Yadav lauded PM Modi's 'Mann Ki Baat' for raising awareness. In the latest episode, the PM celebrated India's wind energy capacity crossing 56 GW, a milestone that positions the country fourth globally in wind power generation.

articles.title: Defence experts warn of fitness tracker risks in Singapore military bases amid global Strava breaches
articles.description: The Ministry of Defence says it is aware of the risks and will implement security measures when needed.

articles.title: Yogi highlights UP Police transformation from strain to strength
articles.description: CM outlines expanded capacity, modern training, commends the increasing role of women recruits at passing-out parade

articles.title: US Iran war leading to advancement of Tehran’s military tech know-how? Explainer on undetonated weapons, diplomacy, red lines and renewed fighting risks
articles.description: US Iran war leading to advancement of Tehrans military tech know-how? Reports say Iran recovered undetonated US missiles and bomblets and moved them to research units for reverse engineering. Officials also warned about risks of miscalculation that could restart conflict. Irans foreign minister travelled to Russia after Pakistan talks and shared messages outlining red lines with the US. The story explains military recovery efforts, diplomatic activity, and regional implications.

articles.title: Haryana to set up social security board for organised workers: Saini
articles.description: Describing workers as the backbone of nation-building, Saini said that the vision of a developed India cannot be realised without their empowerment, dignity and active participation

articles.title: Kejriwal tried to stop defections, but it was a case of too little, too late
articles.description: Kejriwal tried to stop defections, but it was a case of too little, too late

articles.title: UP man taking Home Guard exam on behalf of another candidate gets caught during biometric verification
articles.description: The examination was conducted by the Uttar Pradesh Police Recruitment and Promotion Board in two shifts on April 25, April 26 and April 27.

articles.title: Progressivism's attack dog SPLC blows itself up
articles.description: The Southern Poverty Law Center helped stoke hate by funneling millions of dollars to the Ku Klux Klan, neo-Nazis and other extremists and engaged in criminal activity to cover it up.

articles.title: Odisha govt docs threaten to quit over unfulfilled demands
articles.description: Odisha government doctors, under the Odisha Medical Service Association (OMSA), are threatening to resign en masse due to unresolved issues of pay parity, cadre restructuring, and dynamic assured career progression. Despite government assurances in January, no significant steps have been taken, prompting the doctors to consider mass resignation.

articles.title: Veggies, Not Jhalmuri: Mamata Banerjee's Casual Response To PM Modi's Snack Break
articles.description: Mamata Banerjee held a footmarch in Bhabanipur, where the BJP has fielded Suvendu Adhikari.

articles.title: Iran FM meets Army Chief Munir after returning to Pakistan amid uncertainty over peace talks with US
articles.description: US Iran talks: Iranian FM Abbas Araghchi meets Pakistan Army chief Asim Munir during brief Islamabad stop as uncertainty clouds next round of negotiations. Visit follows Oman talks, Trump remarks, and ongoing regional diplomatic efforts.

articles.title: Activists raise alarm over dairy sector’s hidden climate impact
articles.description: Activists raise alarm over dairy sector’s hidden climate impact

articles.title: Aspirants urge early start to OSSC librarian hiring process
articles.description: Aspiring librarians in Odisha are urging Chief Minister Mohan Charan Majhi to expedite the application process for 31 advertised posts. Despite an indicative notice in December 2025, the detailed notification and application window for librarian positions under the OSSC CGL 2025 recruitment remain open. Candidates express concern over the prolonged delay, seeking clarity and timely commencement of the recruitment drive.

articles.title: JioStar moves Bombay High Court against CCI order in broadcast dispute
articles.description: JioStar moves Bombay High Court, challenging CCI's jurisdiction in Asianet dispute, arguing the matter falls under TRAI's regulatory domain

articles.title: Iran’s foreign minister returns to Islamabad despite US cancelling talks
articles.description: Iran's foreign minister returned to Islamabad on Sunday, ping-ponging from capital to capital as mediators hoped to keep peace talks between Tehran and the United States alive.

articles.title: Magnanimous humility
articles.description: Humility is the art of recognizing our own shortcomings. It invites us to lessen our self-importance, allowing space for others to shine. This graceful humility encourages open conversations free of ego, nurturing empathy and connection. By embracing this mindset, we forge stronger bonds and pave the way for respectful dialogues in the future.

articles.title: ‘Biden cover-up’ hearing | 'Who was the real Prez?': Senators erupt as autopen scandal explodes at
articles.description: Tensions flared during a Senate Judiciary Committee hearing as GOP Senators accused Democrats of covering up concerns over former US President Joe Biden’s cognitive health. Senator Josh Hawley (R-MO) delivered a blistering rebuke, demanding answers on who was truly making decisions in the White House.

articles.title: Iran Presents ‘Amended Proposal Of Framework’ To End US-Israel Conflict: Exclusive
articles.description: If the Muscat meetings and Araghchi’s follow-up Islamabad tour remain fruitful then United States and Iran may resume second round of Islamabad talks soon, said sources

articles.title: PM Modi Visits Thanthania Kalibari Temple Ahead of Kolkata Road Show - In Pics
articles.description: Ahead of the second phase of the West Bengal Assembly Elections 2026, Prime Minister Narendra Modi offered prayers at Thakurbari, the main temple of the Matua Mahasangha at Thakurnagar on Sunday, April 26. The temple visit was followed by the PM's poll rally in the area in North 24 Parganas. Later in the day, he also prayed to Maa Kali, at the Thanthania Kalibari Temple. ​

articles.title: Karnataka leadership change: Chief Minister and me will abide by high command’s decision, says DKS
articles.description: Deputy CM D.K. Shivakumar affirms commitment to Congress leadership decisions amid speculation of changes in Karnataka government.

articles.description: MLC Thippannappa Kamaknoor refutes claims of halted ST certificate issuance for the Talawar community, ensuring genuine applications are processed.

articles.title: PM Modi urges citizens to participate in Census 2027, calls it 'shared responsibility' in 'Mann Ki Baat'
articles.description: Describing the census as the world's largest population exercise, PM Modi highlighted its digital transformation, enhanced public participation through self-enumeration, and its crucial role in strengthening governance and policy-making.

articles.title: ‘Are people of Bengal terrorists?’: Kejriwal questions deployment of security forces during WB polls
articles.description: Arvind Kejriwal criticised the deployment of 3.5 lakh security personnel in West Bengal, alleging fake voters, misuse of agencies, and pressure on Mamata Banerjee.

articles.title: PM Modi visits Matua stronghold as West Bengal electoral battle heats up
articles.description: This is not Modi’s first outreach to the community. He had visited the Thakurnagar shrine in 2019 and also paid homage at Orakandi in Bangladesh in 2021, underlining the political and cultural significance of the Matua connect in the region.

articles.title: Delhi Legislative Assembly to commence one-day special session on April 28
One in three Gen Z men believe a wife should obey her husband: What the research reveals about gender and power
articles.description: A global survey reveals a surprising trend: a significant portion of Gen Z men hold traditional views on marriage and gender roles, contradicting expectations of progressive attitudes. While many admire successful women, a substantial number believe wives should obey husbands and men should have the final say in family decisions, sparking concerns about future gender equality.

articles.title: China’s gameplan for Arunachal Pradesh: Demands a stronger Indian response
articles.description: China has once again released a list of Chinese names for places in Arunachal Pradesh, which it refers to as Zangnan (South Tibet), as part of its territorial claims. The latest batch reportedly covers around...

articles.title: Rahul Gandhi working for BJP, says TMC leader Kunal Ghosh amid political row
articles.description: TMC leader Kunal Ghosh accused Rahul Gandhi of "working for the BJP" after the Congress MP criticized Mamata Banerjee and alleged corruption. Ghosh stated Gandhi failed to stop the BJP in other states and is now aiding them in West Bengal. The remarks followed Gandhi's criticism of both TMC and BJP over corruption and electoral malpractice.

articles.title: Are Trump, Netanyahu in sync? How US and Israel are fighting different wars on same battlefield
articles.description: As a two-month conflict with Iran escalates, cracks are appearing in the US-Israel alliance. While both nations initially targeted Tehran, their post-strike objectives diverge, with Washington seeking negotiation and Jerusalem aiming for regime change. This fundamental difference, exacerbated by past events and differing pressures, raises questions about their long-term alignment.

articles.title: Are live-in relationships better than marriage: How to choose consciously, suggests Sadhguru
articles.description: Are live-in relationships better than marriage: How to choose consciously, suggests Sadhguru

articles.title: 'AAP became symbol of corruption, misgovernance': Piyush Goyal after AAP MPs join BJP
articles.description: Union Minister Piyush Goyal on Sunday reacted to the joining of two-thirds of Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) Rajya Sabha MPs, including Raghav Chadha, with the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP), alleging that the AAP had lost its core values and was associated with corruption and misgovernance.

articles.title: Persian proverb of the day: ‘As long as you are standing, give a hand to those who have fallen’ - a lesson on kindness and human relationships
articles.description: Persian proverb of the day: A Persian proverb, 'As long as you are standing, give a hand to those who have fallen,' emphasizes the importance of compassion and responsibility. It highlights that true strength lies in supporting those struggling, as life's circumstances can change, and kindness in success is paramount.

articles.title: Opinion: Has America's Politics Become A Threat To The Idea Of America?
articles.description: It signals something deeper: the normalisation of extreme rhetoric and the erosion of institutional trust between political camps

articles.title: 'Participation In Census 2027 Is Vital': PM Modi Urges Citizens, Assures Digital Security
articles.description: 'Your Participation In Census 2027 Is Vital': PM Modi Urges Citizens In 'Mann Ki Baat'

articles.title: Tamil Nadu polls: Vijay has instructed candidates to inspect strong room on rotation basis, says TVK leader
articles.description: TVK leader Aadhav Arjuna revealed party chief Vijay directed candidates to monitor EVM strong rooms. Elections concluded on April 23 with high turnout. Candidates will inspect strong rooms on rotation. Arjuna urged transparent counting and adequate security for May 4. He anticipates a democratic victory for TVK, predicting a government change based on people's verdict.

articles.title: Trump’s First Address After Chilling ‘Assassination Attempt’ At Washington Hilton
articles.description: In his first major reveal after the shocking incident at the Washington Hilton, Donald Trump addressed the nation with a tense and measured tone. He described the attacker as a “lone wolf,” but stressed that nothing is confirmed as investigators continue examining every possible angle. The statement, delivered in the aftermath of an alleged assassination attempt, carried a heavy sense of uncertainty and urgency. While authorities piece together the motive and connections, the political atmosphere remains charged, with security fears and unanswered questions hanging over Washington.

articles.title: No peace talks under shadow of blockades and threats: Iran's President Pezeshkian to Pakistan PM Shehbaz Sharif
articles.description: Iran's President Masoud Pezeshkian has refused direct talks with the United States. He stated Iran will not negotiate under pressure or threats. The ongoing naval blockade and hostile US actions are the main obstacles. Pezeshkian urged the US to remove these barriers first. This comes amid stalled peace talks and a US naval blockade.

articles.title: Who Is Pearlman? Man On Stage During Shooting Incident As Trump Was Evacuated
articles.description: Pearlman said his goal was to "bring us together" and give the room 25 minutes of laughter and jaw-dropping moments instead of political jabs.

articles.title: Assembly Elections 2026 Live Updates: CM Stalin taking break in Kodaikanal after intense election work
articles.description: Assembly Elections 2026 Live Updates: After an intensive election campaign tour, Tamil Nadu Chief Minister MK Stalin left for a short trip to Kodaikanal in Dindigul district with his family. During his stay, the Chief Minister on Saturday took a walk around the scenic Kodaikanal Lake, a prominent attraction in the hill town. He was seen interacting with tourists and locals, exchanging greetings, and posing for photographs and selfies.

articles.title: US-Iran war: FM Araghchi to head back to Pakistan after Oman visit
articles.description: US-Iran war news: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi is set for another visit to Pakistan. This trip follows his engagements in Oman and precedes his travel to Russia. A portion of his delegation has returned to Tehran for crucial consultations. These discussions are focused on matters related to ending the ongoing conflict. The delegation is expected to rejoin Araghchi in Islamabad soon.

articles.title: PM Modi reacts to gunfire scare at White House dinner
articles.description: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi conveyed relief following a security incident in Washington DC. US President Donald Trump, the First Lady, and the Vice President were reported safe. An armed individual opened fire at a hotel, causing panic. Law enforcement apprehended the suspect. The event has renewed focus on security at high-profile gatherings.

articles.title: 'Violence has no place in democracy' PM Modi reacts to armed man storming Trump event
articles.description: Indian PM Narendra Modi expressed relief over President Trump’s safety after an armed man stormed a Washington DC hotel during a high-profile event

articles.title: ‘Violence has no place’: PM Modi condemns shooting incident at WH Correspondents’ Dinner; extends well wishes to US President
articles.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has finally reacted to the recent security incident in Washington.

articles.title: ‘Violence has no place’: Modi reacts to Trump’s White House event shooting incident

articles.title: Assembly Elections 2026 Live Updates: PM Modi To Hold Multiple Rallies In Bengal Today Ahead Of April 29 Polls
articles.description: The day will conclude with a roadshow in Kolkata at around 5:30 pm, expected to draw large crowds as the BJP intensifies its campaign efforts in the state.
